title: 14CO2-based assay for measuring Rubisco activity & activation state
author: Cristina Rodrigues Gabriel Sales
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bf8cjrsw

steps.title: Prepare Reagents and Stock Solutions
steps.instruction:
Prepare the following reagents and stock solutions:
- Basic extraction buffer (1x): 50 mM Bicine-NaOH pH 8.2, 20 mM MgCl2.6H2O, 1 mM EDTA, 2 mM Benzamidine, 5 mM ε-Aminocaproic acid. Dissolve in ultrapure H2O, adjust pH to 8.2 with NaOH, degas the solution bubbling with nitrogen (5 min/100 mL), then add 50 mM 2-Mercaptoethanol. Adjust for final volume. Store at -20 °C.
- 100 mM PMSF: Dissolve in ethanol 99%. Store at 4 °C.
- Plant protease inhibitor cocktail: Store at -20 °C.
- Basic assay buffer (2x): 200 mM Bicine-NaOH, 40 mM MgCl2.6H2O. Dissolve in ultrapure H2O, adjust pH to 8.2 with NaOH, adjust for final volume, degas the solution bubbling with nitrogen (5 min/100 mL). Store at -20 °C.
- 30 mM RuBP: High purity (≥99%) is required. Store at -20 °C.
- 0.1 M NaH 14 CO 3 (0.5 Ci/mol): Store at -20 °C.
- 0.3 M KOH: Store at room temperature.
- 10 M Formic acid: Store at room temperature.
- Gold Star Quanta scintillation cocktail: Store at room temperature.
- 1 M DTT: Dissolve in ultrapure H2O. Store at 4 °C.

steps.title: Prepare Complete Working Buffers
steps.instruction:
Prepare the complete extraction buffer (1x) and complete assay buffer with reagents prepared in the previous step.
- Complete extraction buffer 1x: Combine Basic extraction buffer, 10 mM DTT, 1 mM PMSF, and 1% (v/v) Plant protease inhibitor cocktail. Prepare the volume considering the number of extractions to be performed plus two extras. Mix all together. Keep on ice. It is important to ensure that the Rubisco concentration in the assays does not compromise the sensitivity of the assays.
- Complete assay buffer: For a volume per vial or reaction of 250 µL, combine 2x Basic assay buffer (final concentration 1x), 50 µL 100 mM NaH 14 CO 3 (final concentration 10 mM), and 165 µL ultrapure H2O. Prepare the volume considering that activities are measured in duplicates (2 technical replicates). If measuring initial and total activities, account for volume for 4 vials per sample, plus additional volume for at least two Blanks and one extra. Optionally, 2 mM KH2PO4 can be added for field samples to maximise measurable Rubisco activity.

steps.title: Initial Equipment Setup and Thawing
steps.instruction: Thaw all frozen solutions that will be used during the day. Turn on the heat block and set to the desired temperature for Rubisco activity measurements (e.g., 25 °C or 30 °C). Turn on the centrifuge and set to 4 °C. Collect samples from -80 °C into liquid nitrogen. Prepare the complete extraction buffer (from Step 1) and the complete assay buffer (from Step 1) and keep them on ice.

steps.title: Pre-set Up Assay Vials
steps.instruction: Immediately before starting the extraction, place 4 vials in the heat block (for 2 initial and 2 total activity assays). Add 465 µL of complete assay buffer (from Step 1) to each of the 4 vials. Add 10 µL of 30 mM RuBP (from Step 0) to the 2 vials designated for initial activities.

steps.title: Sample Extraction
steps.instruction: Add the complete extraction buffer to an ice-cold mortar. Take a sample from the liquid nitrogen container and add it to the mortar. Grind the sample thoroughly for 30 seconds to a maximum of 1 minute. Collect the homogenate into an ice-cold 1.5 mL microtube and centrifuge at 14000 x g, 4 °C, for 1 minute. To prevent Rubisco deactivation (or even denaturation), the extraction should not take more than 1 minute and it should be done in an ice-cold mortar, keeping the sample cold at all times. When centrifugation stops, transfer the extract supernatant into another ice-cold 1.5 mL microtube.

steps.title: Initiate Rubisco Assays
steps.instruction: Proceed with the Rubisco assays straight away. Add 25 µL of sample extract consecutively to each of the 4 pre-set vials, with 15 second intervals (Total1, Total2, Initial1, Initial2). Ensure all additions are completed within 1.5 minutes of finishing centrifugation. The Initial activity assays start with extract addition. The Total activity assays start with the addition of RuBP after 3 minutes of extract incubation with CO2 and Mg2+ to allow for Rubisco carbamylation. Quench the assays after 30 seconds by adding 100 µL of 10 M formic acid (from Step 0). Repeat this process for all extractions of the day.

steps.title: Prepare Blanks and Radioactivity Checks
steps.instruction: Blanks can be prepared at the start or end of the day by adding to each blank vial 465 µL of complete assay buffer (from Step 1) and 100 µL of 10 M formic acid (from Step 0). Prepare at least two background checks per experiment by adding to each vial 465 µL of complete assay buffer (from Step 1), 25 µL sample extract, and 3 minutes later, 100 µL of 10 M formic acid (from Step 0). For radioactivity verification of the complete assay buffer, add 490 µL of 0.3 M KOH (from Step 0) plus 10 µL of complete assay buffer (from Step 1). Then, add 3.6 mL of Gold Star Quanta scintillation cocktail (from Step 0). Close the vial as soon as possible and mix well.

steps.title: Post-Assay Processing and Counting
steps.instruction: Dry all the vials at 100 °C in the heat block for approximately 1 hour. Let the vials cool, then add 400 µL of ultrapure H2O to each vial to re-hydrate acid-stable compounds. Wait for 5 minutes. Add 3.6 mL of Gold Star Quanta scintillation cocktail (from Step 0). Close the vials and vortex/mix well. Determine 14C radioactivity using a scintillation counter.

steps.title: Rubisco Activity Calculations
steps.instruction: Use the following assumptions and calculations: NaH 14 CO 3: 0.5 Ci/mol CO 2 = 0.5 µCi/µmol CO 2. 1 µCi = 2,220,000 disintegration per minute (dpm). Correct vial DPM values by subtracting background counts (Blank DPM). Convert dpm to µCi (dpm / 2,220,000). Convert to CO2 concentration (µCi / 0.5). Rubisco activity is then converted to the unit of interest by accounting for the reaction time, the volume of sample extract used, and the corresponding sample leaf area (µmol CO2 m-2 s-1) or protein content (µmol CO2 min-1 mg-1). From the Rubisco activity calculations for initial (Vi) and total activity (Vt), the Rubisco activation state (AS, %) can be calculated: AS = 100 × Vi / Vt.
